# Real-world chess score sheet test fixtures

Downloaded 2026-06-19 for use as test fixtures (handwritten/filled-in and blank
chess score sheets / German "Partieformulare").

Most images come from Wikimedia Commons (clear, open licensing). Two German
blank templates were rendered to PNG from publicly downloadable PDFs.

| File | Type | Dimensions | Size | Description |
|------|------|------------|------|-------------|
| sheet-01.jpg | JPEG | 572×787 | 103 KB | Handwritten (historical) |
| sheet-02.jpg | JPEG | 1708×1200 | 496 KB | Handwritten (historical) |
| sheet-03.jpg | JPEG | 4928×3264 | 4.1 MB | Handwritten, real photo on board |
| sheet-04.jpg | JPEG | 4928×3264 | 3.6 MB | Handwritten, real photo on board |
| sheet-05.jpg | JPEG | 1020×1539 | 683 KB | Didactic/example, partly filled |
| sheet-06.jpg | JPEG | 768×1024 | 180 KB | Blank template (Polish) |
| sheet-07.gif | GIF | 493×707 | 188 KB | Handwritten (historical, German notation) |
| sheet-08.png | PNG | 1276×1790 | 510 KB | Blank template (GERMAN, "Partieformular") |
| sheet-09.png | PNG | 1240×1755 | 80 KB | Blank template (GERMAN, "Partieformular") |

---

## sheet-01.jpg
- Source page: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Planilha_Eisenberg_e_Capablanca.jpg
- Direct image URL: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/0/0e/Planilha_Eisenberg_e_Capablanca.jpg
- License: Public Domain (CC PD Mark 1.0)
- Description: Handwritten 1909 score sheet of the game Eisenberg vs. Capablanca,
  notated by Capablanca. Scanned, slightly low resolution. Filled / handwritten.

## sheet-02.jpg
- Source page: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Fischer_Score_Card.jpg
- Direct image URL: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/9/91/Fischer_Score_Card.jpg
- License: Public Domain (deemed ineligible for copyright)
- Description: Handwritten score card from Bobby Fischer's 1970 Chess Olympiad
  game vs. Najdorf (descriptive notation). Good quality scan. Filled / handwritten.

## sheet-03.jpg
- Source page: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Planilla_Carlsen.jpg
- Direct image URL: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/e/e6/Planilla_Carlsen.jpg
- License: CC BY-SA 4.0
- Description: High-resolution real photo of a FIDE World Championship 2018
  score sheet (Carlsen–Caruana, game 9) lying on the board. Handwritten moves
  visible; shot at an angle (perspective distortion) — realistic photo conditions.

## sheet-04.jpg
- Source page: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Planilla_Magnus_Carlsen.jpg
- Direct image URL: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/f/f7/Planilla_Magnus_Carlsen.jpg
- License: CC BY-SA 4.0
- Description: High-resolution real photo of Carlsen's filled FIDE 2018 score
  sheet, similar setup to sheet-03 (different frame/angle). Handwritten.

## sheet-05.jpg
- Source page: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Anotaci%C3%B3n_027.jpg
- Direct image URL: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/b/b6/Anotaci%C3%B3n_027.jpg
- License: CC BY-SA 2.5 CO (Colombia)
- Description: Didactic illustration (Coldeportes, Colombia) showing how to
  annotate chess moves on a score sheet; contains example/sample move entries.
  Partly filled instructional material rather than a real played game.

## sheet-06.jpg
- Source page: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Chess_Score_Sheet.jpg
- Direct image URL: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/3/3f/Chess_Score_Sheet.jpg
- License: Public Domain
- Description: BLANK template, Polish ("Zapis partii szachowej") with multi-column
  move grid (Nr/Białe/Czarne ×3) and header fields Runda/Data/Białe/Czarne/ELO.
  Same structural layout as a German Partieformular. Blank.

## sheet-07.gif
- Source page: https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Planilha_R%C3%A9ti_e_Capablanca.gif
- Direct image URL: https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/f/f6/Planilha_R%C3%A9ti_e_Capablanca.gif
- License: Public Domain
- Description: Handwritten 1924 score sheet of Réti vs. Capablanca in German
  algebraic notation. Historical, filled / handwritten. (GIF format.)

## sheet-08.png
- Source page: https://schach-fuer-kids.de/wp-content/uploads/2019/04/SFK_Partieformular_03.pdf
- Direct source (PDF): https://schach-fuer-kids.de/wp-content/uploads/2019/04/SFK_Partieformular_03.pdf
- License: Not explicitly stated (publicly downloadable from schach-fuer-kids.de).
  Treat as "all rights reserved unless cleared" — use as a test fixture only.
- Description: GERMAN blank "Partieformular" (Schach für Kids). Header fields
  "Spieler Weiß / Spieler Schwarz / Datum / Ergebnis" and a two-column move grid
  (1–50). Rendered from page 1 of the PDF at 150 DPI. Blank template.

## sheet-09.png
- Source page: http://www.jakob.at/steffen/chess/partie_formular.html
- Direct source (PDF): https://jakob.at/data/pdf/Partie-Formular.pdf
- License: Not explicitly stated (publicly downloadable from jakob.at).
  Treat as "all rights reserved unless cleared" — use as a test fixture only.
- Description: GERMAN blank "Partieformular" (Steffen / jakob.at, DIN A4). Header
  fields "Weiß / Schwarz / Veranstaltung / Runde / Brett Nr. / Datum / Eröffnung"
  plus a move grid (1–90) and team-result table (Heimmannschaft/Gastmannschaft/
  Ergebnis). Rendered from page 1 of the PDF at 150 DPI. NOTE: the PDF page is
  laid out in landscape, so this PNG appears rotated 90°. Blank template.

---

## Notes / caveats
- 5 of 9 images are filled / handwritten (sheet-01, -02, -03, -04, -07);
  sheet-05 is didactic/example-filled; sheet-06, -08, -09 are blank templates.
- No real-world FILLED-IN *German* Partieformular photo was found openly licensed.
  The handwritten examples (Wikimedia) are historical/international; the German
  examples available openly are blank templates (sheet-08, -09) and one with
  German notation (sheet-07, Réti–Capablanca 1924).
- sheet-08 and sheet-09 are rendered from PDFs whose license is not explicitly
  stated; they are publicly downloadable and used here only as local test
  fixtures, not redistributed.
